question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `TOPIC_MESSAGES_BY_ID_QUERY_HINT` (`set local random_page_cost = 0`) in `TopicMessageRepositoryCustomImpl.findByFilter()` by sending many concurrent queries, causing PostgreSQL to always choose the secondary index over the primary key for all concurrent queries, leading to index scan contention and database CPU exhaustion DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open many subscriptions with `startTime=0` to a topic with millions of messages, causing `PollingTopicListener.poll()` to call `findByFilter()` which calls `getResultList()` (not a streaming cursor per the comment in the code), loading up to `maxPageSize=5000` records per poll into a `List`, and with many concurrent subscribers each polling independently, exhausting the database connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `onBackpressureBuffer(listenerProperties.getMaxBufferSize(), BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR)` in `SharedTopicListener.listen()` by subscribing with a deliberately slow consumer (e.g., adding artificial processing delay), causing the buffer to fill and `ERROR` to be emitted, which if unhandled propagates as an `OverflowException` that terminates the subscriber's stream and potentially the shared upstream Flux?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open subscriptions to thousands of distinct topic IDs in `PollingTopicListener` (POLL mode), where each subscription creates its own independent `PollingContext` and polling loop on `Schedulers.boundedElastic()`, causing the bounded elastic thread pool (default 10× CPU cores) to be fully saturated, starving all other reactive operations in the JVM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `jitter(0.1)` in `RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE).jitter(0.1)` in `PollingTopicListener.listen()` by opening many subscriptions, causing the jitter to spread poll times across the interval window but still maintaining a high aggregate query rate that overwhelms the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription with `limit=1` to trigger the special case in `TopicMessageRepositoryCustomImpl.findByFilter()` where `filter.getLimit() != 1` is false and the `TOPIC_MESSAGES_BY_ID_QUERY_HINT` is NOT applied, causing the PostgreSQL planner to choose a suboptimal query plan (primary key scan) for a high-volume topic, resulting in slow queries that hold DB connections and cause a connection pool exhaustion DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter))` in `PollingTopicListener.poll()` where the underlying `Stream<TopicMessage>` is backed by a JPA `getResultList()` call that loads all results eagerly into memory, by crafting a filter that matches a large number of records (e.g., `startTime=0`, no limit), causing a single poll to allocate a massive `ArrayList` and trigger an OOM DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open many subscriptions to the same topic in `RedisTopicListener`, where `computeIfAbsent()` returns the cached shared `Flux` but the `container` Mono (lazily initialized Redis connection) is shared and cached via `.cache()`, causing all subscribers to share a single Redis connection that becomes a bottleneck, and if that connection fails, all subscribers are simultaneously disrupted causing a cascading DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `AtomicLong count` in `PollingTopicListener.PollingContext.onNext()` by subscribing to a topic with an extremely high message rate, causing `count.incrementAndGet()` to eventually overflow from `Long.MAX_VALUE` to `Long.MIN_VALUE`, making `filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()` produce a very large positive number and causing `pageSize` to jump to `maxPageSize` unexpectedly, disrupting the polling logic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open many subscriptions with `startTime` set to the current time minus 1 nanosecond, causing each `PollingTopicListener` subscription to immediately start polling from near-current time, and with many concurrent subscribers each holding a `boundedElastic` thread and a DB connection, exhausting both the thread pool and the database connection pool simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `SharedPollingTopicListener` constructor which initializes `topicMessages` as a single shared `Flux` with `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)`, by causing repeated DB failures that trigger the retry loop, where each retry attempt re-subscribes to the shared Flux and re-initializes `PollingContext.onStart()`, resetting `lastConsensusTimestamp` to `DomainUtils.now()` and causing all in-flight subscribers to miss messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open many subscriptions with `prefetch=4` (minimum allowed) to a high-throughput topic in `SharedTopicListener.listen()`, causing `publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, 4)` to create many small-batch dispatch tasks on the bounded elastic scheduler, generating excessive task scheduling overhead that saturates the scheduler and causes a CPU DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `filterMessage()` method in `CompositeTopicListener` which performs `message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId())` for every message on the shared stream, by subscribing many clients to different topic IDs on a `SHARED_POLL` listener, causing O(N×M) filter evaluations (N subscribers × M messages per poll) that exhaust CPU and cause a compute-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription with `startTime=0` and `limit=Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `PollingTopicListener.poll()` to compute `(int)(Long.MAX_VALUE - 0)` which overflows to `-1` when cast to `int`, and `Math.min(-1, maxPageSize)` returns `-1`, causing `typedQuery.setMaxResults(-1)` which in JPA/Hibernate removes the result limit entirely, loading the full table into memory and causing an OOM DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `doOnSubscribe(s -> log.info(...))` in `PollingTopicListener.listen()` by opening thousands of subscriptions per second, causing the logger to emit thousands of INFO-level log messages per second, overwhelming the logging subsystem (e.g., async log queue), causing log queue overflow and potential application thread blocking?
